tool_design.role_in_intervention: Quarterly audit that tests whether the live allergen source-of-truth still matches real menu items, supplier evidence, and customer-facing materials.
tool_design.usage_moment: Quarterly, after significant menu change, or after any allergen near-miss
tool_design.expected_output: Completed scored audit with sampled item trace, findings, corrective actions, and recheck dates

failure_warnings.warning: The audit becomes a box-tick review of paperwork rather than a check of live reality.
failure_warnings.what_can_go_wrong: The venue audits the files while real service has already drifted.
early_signals: No live menu items are sampled during the audit
The audit reads documents but never checks current stock or service copies
prevention: Trace sample items from sale point to evidence source every quarter
Include specials, bought-in products, and one service point in the sample
mitigation: Re-run the audit with live item tracing
Prioritise corrective action on the sampled gaps before broad paperwork cleanup

failure_warnings.warning: Findings are documented but not converted into source updates and follow-up dates.
failure_warnings.what_can_go_wrong: The same gaps recur every quarter and the audit loses value.
early_signals: Past findings appear again with no closure note
Corrective actions have no owner or date
prevention: Require named ownership and due dates on every material finding
Review the prior audit before starting the new one
mitigation: Escalate repeat findings into B008 programme review
Set a short recheck date for unresolved high-risk items

content:
# Quarterly Allergen Source-of-Truth Audit

**Tool ID:** T005-06  
**Block:** B005 (Allergen Identification & Labelling)  
**Format:** Audit  
**Primary Role:** Owner  
**Use Moment:** Quarterly and after significant allergen changes or near-misses  
**Output:** Scored audit with corrective actions and recheck dates

---

## What This Tool Does

This audit checks whether the allergen source-of-truth still matches live reality. It should test the chain from menu item to evidence, not just whether documents exist.

Sample at least:
- 2 permanent menu items
- 1 bought-in bakery or packaged item
- 1 special or seasonal item if active
- 1 modifier, topping, syrup, or garnish

---

## Audit Header

| Field | Complete Here |
|---|---|
| Audit date | |
| Auditor | |
| Venue | |
| Matrix version audited | |
| Previous audit date | |
| Previous open findings reviewed? | Yes / No |

---

## Sample Item Trace

| Sampled Item | Seen Live in Service? | Matrix Row Current? | Declaration Sheet Found? | Supplier Evidence Found? | Customer Pack Matches? | Result |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| | Yes / No | Yes / No | Yes / No | Yes / No / N/A | Yes / No | Pass / Fail |
| | Yes / No | Yes / No | Yes / No | Yes / No / N/A | Yes / No | Pass / Fail |
| | Yes / No | Yes / No | Yes / No | Yes / No / N/A | Yes / No | Pass / Fail |
| | Yes / No | Yes / No | Yes / No | Yes / No / N/A | Yes / No | Pass / Fail |
| | Yes / No | Yes / No | Yes / No | Yes / No / N/A | Yes / No | Pass / Fail |

---

## System Scores

| Area | Score 1-5 | Evidence / Finding |
|---|---|---|
| Master matrix completeness | | |
| Recipe declaration quality | | |
| Supplier evidence currency | | |
| Change-control compliance | | |
| Customer-facing pack accuracy | | |
| Version control and document replacement | | |

**Scoring guide:** `5 = Strong and current` | `3 = Meets minimum but weak` | `1 = Unsafe or unreliable`

---

## Corrective Actions

| Finding | Risk Level | Action Required | Owner | Due Date | Recheck Date |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| | High / Medium / Low | | | | |
| | High / Medium / Low | | | | |
| | High / Medium / Low | | | | |
| | High / Medium / Low | | | | |

---

## Trigger Escalation If

<input type="checkbox" disabled /> A live item cannot be traced to evidence  
<input type="checkbox" disabled /> A bought-in product is in service with no current declaration  
<input type="checkbox" disabled /> Customer-facing sheets do not match the current matrix  
<input type="checkbox" disabled /> Repeat findings remain open from the prior quarter

---

## Common Failure Patterns

### 1. Auditing paperwork instead of service reality
Physically check a live menu item, the stock in use, and the copy available to staff.

### 2. Sampling only easy or stable items
Use at least one high-change or bought-in item every quarter.

### 3. Closing the audit without recheck dates
A finding without a recheck date is usually just a note, not a correction plan.
